summary: Delete the copy-tree scratch import pipeline (import::run, the surface writers, write_rollup, emit_manifest, carry_representation, scratch_surface); the discovery walk survives as the sole extractor

per.section: Decision: one authored surface, one implementation

edit.description: Delete `run`/`run_with_builtins` (import.rs:143/154), the copy-tree writers `write_member_surface`/`existing_surface_member`/`copy_companion` (621/670/840), `write_rollup` (866 — its transitional-producer job ended in S3), and `emit_manifest` (426). KEEP `discover_kind_units`/`discover_builtin` (713/569) — the discovery walk is the sole extractor. `init`/`lift` (254/337) survive to S5.

Retire `carry_representation` (frontmatter.rs:318) and the custom-header carry twin — the authored-layer carry-forward is dead once members come from the lock + governs walk, not a re-imported surface.

S2 shipped the lock-read gate but left the `Check --harness` branch (main.rs:286) and `session_start_diagnostics` (main.rs:583,589) on the transitional scratch producer (`scratch_surface` → `import::run` → `gate`). Rewire both to gate the harness root directly — `gate` already walks each built-in kind's embedded `governs` (the built-in lock via `effective_governs`) when no committed lock exists, so no scratch import is needed. Then delete `scratch_surface` (main.rs:1183) and both former call sites.

tests.asserts: emit + check operate with no copy-tree scratch import; the discovery walk is the only member extractor

acceptance: `import::run`, the copy-tree surface writers, `write_rollup`, `emit_manifest`, `carry_representation`, and `scratch_surface` are gone; the discovery walk (`discover_kind_units`/`discover_builtin`) survives; `cargo test` green.
notes: Clean slate (John 07-06): no compat shim. Shares import.rs/frontmatter.rs/main.rs with the chain — serialized behind S3, which removed the last `import::run` test callers. `init`/`lift` deletion waits for S5 (the codec they drive).

summary: Retire the [[member]] manifest codec both directions plus AuthorLayer's member readers, and remove the init/lift verbs (their only remaining callers) under clean slate

per.section: Decision: install is the front door; the lift is its yes-path; the postures stay equal

edit.description: Retire the `[[member]]` codec both directions: writers `write_members`/`member_to_table` and the genre/section sub-writers (compose.rs:1512-1656), and parsers `parse_member`/`parse_member_fields`/`parse_member_sections`/`parse_member_fenced`/`parse_member_genres`/`parse_member_published` (1738-1980). Retire `AuthorLayer::members()`/`inplace_members()` (their consumers die with init/lift). Keep the `[role.*]`/severity layer if still read elsewhere; S7 finishes it.
Remove the `init`/`lift` verbs (import.rs:254/337) — the codec's only remaining callers. Clean slate: a temporary on-ramp gap on trunk is acceptable until S6 ships the front door.
Drop the `Command::Init` variant + dispatch (main.rs:256) and its clap definition; `install` is the sole on-ramp (S6). No `init` alias survives (clean slate).

tests.asserts: genre leaf addressing survives without the [[member]] manifest round-trip block

acceptance: The `[[member]]` codec (both directions), `AuthorLayer`'s member readers, and the `init`/`lift` verbs are gone; `temper.toml` as a manifest dialect is unreadable; `cargo test` green (tests/temper_toml.rs deleted, genre_leaf's manifest block edited out).
notes: Clean slate (John 07-06): `init` may be removed before the front door ships — a trunk on-ramp gap is acceptable, green gates the only bar. Shares compose.rs/import.rs/main.rs — serialized behind S4. genre_leaf.rs manifest round-trip block trimmed here.

summary: Rebuild install as the one on-ramp: discovery report → one question → scaffold the SDK program + first emit on yes, reporter alone on no; guard/note grounded in the lock

per.section: install — the front door; the lift, once

edit.description: (a) Scaffolder: discovery walk → generated TS member modules (`file()` pointers at the original paths, bytes untouched) + `harness.ts` skeleton — the lift's output is the SDK program and nothing else, no lock. (b) Verb: discovery report first, one question 'represent this project?' (`--yes` for agents/CI, `--no-represent`); no-path = consented SessionStart reporter alone (Node-free); yes-path = require Node+`.temper` (hard refusal with instructions when absent), ensure `@dtmd/temper` dep, scaffold, run first emit (the adoption moment), place per lock. (c) Guard/note lock-grounded: rewrite `GUARD_MESSAGE` (install.rs:101) and the managed-by note off the lock's emit-owned rows; place the modeline only when its schema artifact exists, the guard only when emit-owned paths exist ('the guard arrives with its constituency').
`Command::Install` dispatch carries the one-question flow (`--yes`/`--no-represent`); `Command::Guard`'s posture reads the lock, not a `temper.toml` AuthorLayer (main.rs:390).

tests.asserts: install reports findings, asks once; no-path wires only the session-start reporter; yes-path scaffolds the SDK program, runs first emit, places note/guard only for lock-declared emit-owned paths; an all-in-place member gets no guard claim

acceptance: `temper install` is the sole on-ramp: report → one question → reporter-alone on no, or scaffold+first-emit+lock-grounded placements on yes; guard/note bind only lock-declared emit-owned paths; a `file()`-own-path member is authored territory (no guard). Cascade's report (1) dissolves.
notes: Resolves `(carriage-aware-placements)` in code (the lock names a path a projection — 20-surface 'surface authority is a declared posture'). Depends on the built-in lock (S1) + lock-read gate (S2) + codec/init removal (S5). Shares install.rs/main.rs — serialized behind S5.

summary: Eradicate every remaining temper.toml reader — AuthorLayer load/authority/fold_local, the TEMPER_TOML const, explain's load_layer residue — the manifest filename is gone

per.section: The lock and drift — one vocabulary

edit.description: Retire `AuthorLayer::load`/`authority`/`fold_local` and the `TEMPER_TOML` const — no verb reads a `temper.toml`. Any `[role.*]`/severity layer S5 left standing retires here if nothing reads it.
Remove the `TEMPER_TOML` usages in `explain` (main.rs:439, `load_layer`) and any guard/check residue — posture and layers come from the lock.

tests.asserts: no verb reads or writes a temper.toml; a file by that name is inert (old temper, never current temper's input)

acceptance: `rg -c 'temper\.toml' src/ tests/` = 0; the manifest filename is retired entirely (20-surface: 'the name temper.toml retires with the manifest era entirely'). Cascade re-onboards via the new install; its manifest-era artifacts are old temper by definition.
notes: Chain terminal. Cascade's reports (2)-(4) — the accepted-debt DATUM under `(inplace-lock-producer)` — close with this slice: no manifest-mode coverage remains. Shares compose.rs/main.rs — serialized behind S6.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, the engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.2, PR #4); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.2 07-06); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. USPTO name screen due before launch.
